allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking uses decomposition to expose interlinked parts and machine-level causality instead of treating the problem as one blob.
allies.source_quote: Decomposition is a fundamental component of understanding complex systems. It helps identify component parts and how they interlink.

allies.rationale_text: Prioritization turns a decomposed tree into a decision about which branches matter enough to keep and which to prune.
allies.source_quote: Once a problem is disaggregated, effective strategy demands prioritization. Tools like the Pareto Principle rely on decomposition to determine which branches are truly important and should be focused on, and which can be pruned.

antagonists.rationale_text: First-principles-thinking resists decomposition when entrenched branch frameworks smuggle assumptions back into the problem.
antagonists.source_quote: If decomposition becomes routine and relies on entrenched conceptual frameworks, it can inadvertently introduce assumptions derived from those frameworks, thereby blocking the radical originality sought by true first principles thinking.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Decomposition conflicts with systems-thinking when breaking a problem into parts destroys the emergent properties and feedback loops that only exist in the whole system.
structured_tensions.source_quote: decomposition conflicts with systems-thinking (systems thinking) when breaking a problem into parts destroys the emergent properties and feedback loops that only exist in the whole system.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Decomposition conflicts with combinatorial-effects when isolating components hides interaction effects that produce the dominant outcome only in combination.
structured_tensions.source_quote: decomposition conflicts with combinatorial-effects (combinatorial effects) when isolating components hides interaction effects that produce the dominant outcome only in combination.

curation_notes.summary: Decomposition is strong on ally structure and one clear first-order conflict, but it does not have an explicit structured tension block to preserve.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor topology or multihop suggestions because this run is frozen at first-order relation semantics.
open_questions: The raw source also implies a big-picture conflict, but there is no clean current canonical target id for that tension.
